tema: A1 Review — Identity: To Be, Pronouns & Demonstratives

banco_oraciones.oracion: This is my friend Kenji. He is from Japan.
Sofía and I are designers. Our office is in Medellín.
These are my parents. Their names are Ana and Luis.
That woman is Carmen. She is Sofía's grandmother.
Is this your camera? Yes, it is.
We are not tourists. We are students.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: This is Sofía. Her is from Colombia.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: This is Sofía. She is from Colombia.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: We combine 'to be', pronouns and this/that/these/those to introduce and identify people and things — the language of meeting someone.

ejercicios.pregunta: ___ is my friend Kenji. He is from Japan.
Sofía and I ___ designers. ___ office is in Medellín.
That is Kenji's camera. ___ is Japanese.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'These' is for one thing near you.

ejercicios.texto: Those women are teachers. ___ names are Ana and Luz.
Kenji is my friend. ___ is from Tokyo.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: This is Sofía. Her is a designer.
ejercicios.respuesta: This is Sofía. She is a designer.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I am Kenji. Mi family is in Japan.
ejercicios.respuesta: I am Kenji. My family is in Japan.

ejercicios.pregunta: Who is your best friend? Answer with 'My best friend is...'
Introduce yourself: name and nationality. (I am... / My name is...)
ejercicios.respuesta: I am Sofía and I am Colombian.

guion: Sofía: Abuela, this is my friend Kenji. He is from Japan.
Carmen: Welcome, Kenji! Our house is your house.
Kenji: Thank you! Is this your garden? It is beautiful.
Carmen: Yes, it is. These flowers are my treasure.
Kenji: Your garden is amazing. My grandmother in Japan is a gardener too!
Carmen: Then we are friends already.

reading.texto: This is our group. Sofía is a designer from Medellín, and her desk is always perfectly organized. Kenji is a student from Japan; his questions are famous in class. Ben is British — those big boots by the door are his. And that wise woman in the kitchen? She is Carmen, Sofía's grandmother. Their house is small, but their hearts are enormous. We are not just classmates. We are a family.
